data-testid="conversation-turn-2" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h4 class="sr-only select-none">ChatGPT said:</h4><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:var(--thread-content-margin-xs,calc(var(--spacing)*4))] @w-sm/main:[--thread-content-margin:var(--thread-content-margin-sm,calc(var(--spacing)*6))] @w-lg/main:[--thread-content-margin:var(--thread-content-margin-lg,calc(var(--spacing)*16))] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] @w-lg/main:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col gap-4 grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="ac8c012c-08e1-4925-b82d-07ce71e95c61" dir="auto" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5-mini" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-1"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="350">A <strong data-start="2" data-end="40">signed data authenticity statement</strong> is a formal declaration, usually in written or electronic form, that confirms the data being presented is <strong data-start="147" data-end="184">accurate, complete, and unaltered</strong>, and that it originates from a legitimate source. The statement is <strong data-start="252" data-end="262">signed</strong> by an authorized individual (or entity), which serves as a verification of the claim.</p>
<p data-start="352" data-end="364">Key aspects:</p>
<ol data-start="366" data-end="1330">
<li data-start="366" data-end="509">
<p data-start="369" data-end="383"><strong data-start="369" data-end="380">Purpose</strong>:</p>
<ul data-start="387" data-end="509">
<li data-start="387" data-end="445">
<p data-start="389" data-end="445">To certify that the data is authentic and trustworthy.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="449" data-end="509">
<p data-start="451" data-end="509">To provide accountability for the integrity of the data.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="511" data-end="790">
<p data-start="514" data-end="529"><strong data-start="514" data-end="526">Contents</strong>:</p>
<ul data-start="533" data-end="790">
<li data-start="533" data-end="604">
<p data-start="535" data-end="604">Identification of the data (dataset name, version, date collected).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="608" data-end="653">
<p data-start="610" data-end="653">A statement of accuracy and completeness.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="657" data-end="706">
<p data-start="659" data-end="706">The scope or limitations of the data, if any.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="710" data-end="790">
<p data-start="712" data-end="790">Signature, name, position, and date of the person or authority verifying it.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="792" data-end="1149">
<p data-start="795" data-end="811"><strong data-start="795" data-end="808">Use Cases</strong>:</p>
<ul data-start="815" data-end="1149">
<li data-start="815" data-end="879">
<p data-start="817" data-end="879">Research publications to verify experimental or survey data.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="883" data-end="972">
<p data-start="885" data-end="972">Corporate reporting or audits where financial or operational data needs verification.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="976" data-end="1025">
<p data-start="978" data-end="1025">Submission of data to regulatory authorities.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1029" data-end="1149">
<p data-start="1031" data-end="1149">Machine learning datasets, where a responsible party confirms that the data is genuine and has not been manipulated.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1151" data-end="1330">
<p data-start="1154" data-end="1165"><strong data-start="1154" data-end="1162">Form</strong>:</p>
<ul data-start="1169" data-end="1330">
<li data-start="1169" data-end="1228">
<p data-start="1171" data-end="1228">Physical (handwritten signature on a printed statement)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1232" data-end="1330">
<p data-start="1234" data-end="1330">Digital (electronic signature, cryptographic signing, or secure document verification methods)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<p data-start="1332" data-end="1466">Essentially, it provides <strong data-start="1357" data-end="1393">legal and professional assurance</strong> that the dataset or information is authentic, traceable, and reliable.</p>
<p data-start="1468" data-end="1592" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">If you want, I can draft a <strong data-start="1495" data-end="1548">template for a signed data authenticity statement</strong> that you could use for a dataset or report.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="z-0 flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div aria-label="Response actions" class="touch:-me-2 data-testid="conversation-turn-4" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h4 class="sr-only select-none">ChatGPT said:</h4><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:var(--thread-content-margin-xs,calc(var(--spacing)*4))] @w-sm/main:[--thread-content-margin:var(--thread-content-margin-sm,calc(var(--spacing)*6))] @w-lg/main:[--thread-content-margin:var(--thread-content-margin-lg,calc(var(--spacing)*16))] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] @w-lg/main:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col gap-4 grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="bb7d1ab9-073c-449b-b24f-a2aeabcaf119" dir="auto" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5-mini" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-1"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="276">The <strong data-start="4" data-end="39">UCI Machine Learning Repository</strong> itself does <strong data-start="52" data-end="101">not issue signed data authenticity statements</strong>. Datasets there are generally provided “as-is” for research and educational purposes, with metadata and source references, but without formal certification of authenticity.</p>
<p data-start="278" data-end="373">Here’s what you can do if you need something equivalent to a signed statement for UCI datasets:</p>
<hr data-start="375" data-end="378">
<h3 data-start="380" data-end="422"><span role="text">1. <strong data-start="387" data-end="422">Check the Dataset Documentation</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="423" data-end="730">
<li data-start="423" data-end="675">
<p data-start="425" data-end="454">Each dataset on UCI includes:</p>
<ul data-start="457" data-end="675">
<li data-start="457" data-end="508">
<p data-start="459" data-end="508">The <strong data-start="463" data-end="492">source or original author</strong> of the dataset.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="511" data-end="572">
<p data-start="513" data-end="572">Publication references (journal papers, technical reports).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="575" data-end="675">
<p data-start="577" data-end="675">Data description files (<code data-start="601" data-end="609">.names</code>, <code data-start="611" data-end="618">.data</code>, <code data-start="620" data-end="626">.txt</code>) which describe features and collection methods.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="676" data-end="730">
<p data-start="678" data-end="730">These can serve as evidence of the dataset’s origin.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="732" data-end="735">
<h3 data-start="737" data-end="773"><span role="text">2. <strong data-start="744" data-end="773">Cite the Dataset Properly</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="774" data-end="1107">
<li data-start="774" data-end="892">
<p data-start="776" data-end="892">When using the dataset in research, include a citation to the original source or paper listed in UCI’s dataset page.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="893" data-end="1107">
<p data-start="895" data-end="917">Example (APA style):</p>
<pre class="overflow-visible! px-0!" data-start="920" data-end="1107"><div class="relative w-full mt-4 mb-1"><div class=""><div class="relative"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0" style="width: 100%; transform: none;"><div class="border border-token-border-light border-radius-3xl corner-superellipse/1.1 rounded-3xl"><div class="h-full w-full border-radius-3xl bg-token-bg-elevated-secondary corner-superellipse/1.1 overflow-clip rounded-3xl lxnfua_clipPathFallback"><div class="pointer-events-none absolute end-1.5 top-1 z-2 md:end-2 md:top-1"><button class="flex gap-1 items-center select-none pointer-events-auto py-2 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-black/5 dark:hover:bg-white/10 size-9 rounded-full px-2" aria-label="Copy" data-state="closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true" class="icon-md"><use href="/cdn/assets/sprites-core-cohbnghc.svg#ce3544" fill="currentColor"></use></svg></button></div><div class="pe-11 pt-3" style="transform: none;"><div class="relative z-0 flex max-w-full"><div id="code-block-viewer" dir="ltr" class="q9tKkq_viewer cm-editor z-10 light:cm-light dark:cm-light flex h-full w-full flex-col items-stretch ͼk ͼy"><div class="cm-scroller"><div class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly"><span>Dua, D., &amp; Graff, C. (2019). UCI Machine Learning Repository [http://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml]. Irvine, CA: University of California, School of Information and Computer Science.</span></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class=""><div class="" style="width: 100%; transform: none;"></div></div></div></div></div></pre>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1109" data-end="1112">
<h3 data-start="1114" data-end="1157"><span role="text">3. <strong data-start="1121" data-end="1157">Create Your Own Signed Statement</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="1158" data-end="1303">If you need a formal signed data authenticity statement for submission or compliance, you can create one <strong data-start="1263" data-end="1284">as the researcher</strong> using the dataset:</p>
<p data-start="1305" data-end="1326"><strong data-start="1305" data-end="1325">Example Template</strong>:</p>
<pre class="overflow-visible! px-0!" data-start="1328" data-end="1789"><div class="relative w-full mt-4 mb-1"><div class=""><div class="relative"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0" style="width: 100%; transform: none;"><div class="border border-token-border-light border-radius-3xl corner-superellipse/1.1 rounded-3xl"><div class="h-full w-full border-radius-3xl bg-token-bg-elevated-secondary corner-superellipse/1.1 overflow-clip rounded-3xl lxnfua_clipPathFallback"><div class="pointer-events-none absolute end-1.5 top-1 z-2 md:end-2 md:top-1"><button class="flex gap-1 items-center select-none pointer-events-auto py-2 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-black/5 dark:hover:bg-white/10 size-9 rounded-full px-2" aria-label="Copy" data-state="closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true" class="icon-md"><use href="/cdn/assets/sprites-core-cohbnghc.svg#ce3544" fill="currentColor"></use></svg></button></div><div class="pe-11 pt-3" style="transform: none;"><div class="relative z-0 flex max-w-full"><div id="code-block-viewer" dir="ltr" class="q9tKkq_viewer cm-editor z-10 light:cm-light dark:cm-light flex h-full w-full flex-col items-stretch ͼk ͼy"><div class="cm-scroller"><div class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly"><span>Data Authenticity Statement</span><br><br><span>I, [Your Name], hereby certify that the dataset titled "[Dataset Name]" obtained from the UCI Machine Learning Repository</span><br><span>(https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/datasets/[Dataset URL]) has been used in its original form without unauthorized modification. </span><br><span>The data is sourced from [Original Authors/Source], as referenced on the UCI repository.</span><br><br><span>Signed:</span><br><br><span>___________________________</span><br><span>[Your Name]</span><br><span>[Your Position / Institution]</span><br><span>[Date]</span></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class=""><div class="" style="width: 100%; transform: none;"></div></div></div></div></div></pre>
<p data-start="1791" data-end="1923">This makes it clear <strong data-start="1811" data-end="1879">you are attesting to the authenticity of the data you downloaded</strong> rather than UCI itself issuing a signature.</p>
<hr data-start="1925" data-end="1928">
<h3 data-start="1930" data-end="1983"><span role="text">4. <strong data-start="1937" data-end="1983">Optional: Verification via Original Author</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="1984" data-end="2296">
<li data-start="1984" data-end="2151">
<p data-start="1986" data-end="2151">If absolute formal authenticity is required, you could try contacting the <strong data-start="2060" data-end="2088">original dataset authors</strong> (often listed on the dataset page) and request verification.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2152" data-end="2296">
<p data-start="2154" data-end="2296">Many authors may provide a confirmation email or letter stating the dataset is legitimate, which can serve as a signed authenticity statement.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2298" data-end="2301">
<p data-start="2303" data-end="2498">If you want, I can make a <strong data-start="2329" data-end="2430">ready-to-use, formal signed data authenticity statement specifically tailored for any UCI dataset</strong> you’re using, so you can just fill in your details and submit it.</p>

class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-1"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="360">A <strong data-start="2" data-end="27">dashboard explanation</strong> refers to a clear description of the purpose, structure, and contents of a <strong data-start="103" data-end="116">dashboard</strong>—a visual interface that organizes and displays key information and metrics for monitoring, analysis, or decision-making. The explanation helps users understand <strong data-start="277" data-end="357">what the dashboard shows, how to interpret it, and how to use it effectively</strong>.</p>
<h3 data-start="362" data-end="406">Key Elements of a Dashboard Explanation:</h3>
<ol data-start="408" data-end="1744">
<li data-start="408" data-end="588">
<p data-start="411" data-end="436"><strong data-start="411" data-end="434">Purpose / Objective</strong></p>
<ul data-start="440" data-end="588">
<li data-start="440" data-end="479">
<p data-start="442" data-end="479">Describes why the dashboard exists.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="483" data-end="588">
<p data-start="485" data-end="588">Example: “This dashboard tracks monthly sales performance and identifies trends in product categories.”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="590" data-end="775">
<p data-start="593" data-end="611"><strong data-start="593" data-end="609">Data Sources</strong></p>
<ul data-start="615" data-end="775">
<li data-start="615" data-end="710">
<p data-start="617" data-end="710">Identifies where the data comes from (databases, APIs, spreadsheets, survey results, etc.).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="714" data-end="775">
<p data-start="716" data-end="775">Notes any data update frequency (real-time, daily, weekly).</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="777" data-end="960">
<p data-start="780" data-end="804"><strong data-start="780" data-end="802">Key Metrics / KPIs</strong></p>
<ul data-start="808" data-end="960">
<li data-start="808" data-end="873">
<p data-start="810" data-end="873">Lists the primary metrics displayed and what each represents.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="877" data-end="960">
<p data-start="879" data-end="960">Example: “Revenue, number of new customers, churn rate, and average order value.”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="962" data-end="1167">
<p data-start="965" data-end="988"><strong data-start="965" data-end="986">Visual Components</strong></p>
<ul data-start="992" data-end="1167">
<li data-start="992" data-end="1047">
<p data-start="994" data-end="1047">Explains charts, graphs, tables, and other visuals.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1051" data-end="1167">
<p data-start="1053" data-end="1167">Example: “The line chart shows revenue trends over the last 12 months; the bar chart compares product categories.”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1169" data-end="1367">
<p data-start="1172" data-end="1194"><strong data-start="1172" data-end="1192">User Interaction</strong></p>
<ul data-start="1198" data-end="1367">
<li data-start="1198" data-end="1276">
<p data-start="1200" data-end="1276">Describes interactive elements (filters, drill-downs, hover-over details).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1280" data-end="1367">
<p data-start="1282" data-end="1367">Example: “Users can filter data by region or product line to see customized metrics.”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1369" data-end="1561">
<p data-start="1372" data-end="1396"><strong data-start="1372" data-end="1394">Insights / Actions</strong></p>
<ul data-start="1400" data-end="1561">
<li data-start="1400" data-end="1463">
<p data-start="1402" data-end="1463">Indicates what insights can be drawn and potential actions.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1467" data-end="1561">
<p data-start="1469" data-end="1561">Example: “High churn rates in Q2 suggest a need for improved customer retention strategies.”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1563" data-end="1744">
<p data-start="1566" data-end="1591"><strong data-start="1566" data-end="1589">Limitations / Notes</strong></p>
<ul data-start="1595" data-end="1744">
<li data-start="1595" data-end="1660">
<p data-start="1597" data-end="1660">Mentions any known constraints, missing data, or assumptions.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1664" data-end="1744">
<p data-start="1666" data-end="1744">Example: “Data excludes offline sales and may have a one-day reporting delay.”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="1746" data-end="1749">
<p data-start="1751" data-end="1943">Essentially, a dashboard explanation functions as <strong data-start="1801" data-end="1843">a guide for interpreting the dashboard</strong>, ensuring users can accurately read the visuals and take appropriate decisions based on the data.</p>
<p data-start="1945" data-end="2114" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">If you want, I can draft a <strong data-start="1972" data-end="2028">concise, professional dashboard explanation template</strong> that can be applied to any business or research dashboard. Do you want me to do that?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="z-0 flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div aria-label="Response actions" class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap <hr data-start="447" data-end="450">
<h3 data-start="452" data-end="497"><span role="text"><strong data-start="456" data-end="497">1. Income Prediction (Classification)</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="498" data-end="908">
<li data-start="498" data-end="613">
<p data-start="500" data-end="613"><strong data-start="500" data-end="513">Objective</strong>: Predict whether an individual earns over $50K per year based on demographic and employment data.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="614" data-end="730">
<p data-start="616" data-end="730"><strong data-start="616" data-end="630">Techniques</strong>: Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Random Forests, Gradient Boosting, SVM, or Neural Networks.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="731" data-end="908">
<p data-start="733" data-end="755"><strong data-start="733" data-end="754">Possible Analysis</strong>:</p>
<ul data-start="758" data-end="908">
<li data-start="758" data-end="832">
<p data-start="760" data-end="832">Feature importance to identify which attributes most influence income.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="835" data-end="908">
<p data-start="837" data-end="908">Confusion matrix, accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score evaluation.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="910" data-end="913">
<h3 data-start="915" data-end="948"><span role="text"><strong data-start="919" data-end="948">2. Socioeconomic Patterns</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="949" data-end="1280">
<li data-start="949" data-end="1044">
<p data-start="951" data-end="1044"><strong data-start="951" data-end="964">Objective</strong>: Explore how demographic factors relate to income, work hours, or occupation.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1045" data-end="1280">

<ul data-start="1072" data-end="1280">
<li data-start="1072" data-end="1145">
<p data-start="1074" data-end="1145">Average income by <strong data-start="1092" data-end="1142">education level, occupation, or marital status</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1148" data-end="1216">
<p data-start="1150" data-end="1216">Compare income distribution between <strong data-start="1186" data-end="1213">male and female workers</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1219" data-end="1280">
<p data-start="1221" data-end="1280">Explore correlations between <strong data-start="1250" data-end="1279">hours-per-week and income</strong>.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1282" data-end="1285">
<h3 data-start="1287" data-end="1324"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1291" data-end="1324">3. Gender and Racial Wage Gap</strong></span></h3>

<hr data-start="2624" data-end="2627">
<h3 data-start="2629" data-end="2669"><span role="text"><strong data-start="2633" data-end="2669">7. Risk Profiling / Segmentation</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="2670" data-end="3010">
<li data-start="2670" data-end="2771">
<p data-start="2672" data-end="2771"><strong data-start="2672" data-end="2685">Objective</strong>: Segment individuals into groups with similar income or employment characteristics.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2772" data-end="2864">
<p data-start="2774" data-end="2864"><strong data-start="2774" data-end="2788">Techniques</strong>: Clustering methods like <strong data-start="2814" data-end="2861">K-Means, Hierarchical Clustering, or DBSCAN</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2865" data-end="3010">
<p data-start="2867" data-end="2880"><strong data-start="2867" data-end="2879">Use Case</strong>:</p>
<ul data-start="2883" data-end="3010">
<li data-start="2883" data-end="2944">
<p data-start="2885" data-end="2944">Create profiles of “high-income” vs. “low-income” adults.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2947" data-end="3010">
<p data-start="2949" data-end="3010">Analyze demographic or occupational patterns in each cluster.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="3012" data-end="3015">
<h3 data-start="3017" data-end="3064"><span role="text"><strong data-start="3021" data-end="3064">8. Predictive vs. Descriptive Analytics</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="3065" data-end="3218">
<li data-start="3065" data-end="3218">
<p data-start="3067" data-end="3108"><strong data-start="3067" data-end="3080">Objective</strong>: Combine both approaches:</p>
<ul data-start="3111" data-end="3218">
<li data-start="3111" data-end="3147">
<p data-start="3113" data-end="3147">Predict income (classification).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3150" data-end="3218">
<p data-start="3152" data-end="3218">Provide descriptive insights (patterns, trends, visualizations).</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>

<h3 data-start="149" data-end="177"><span role="text"><strong data-start="153" data-end="177">Introduction (Draft)</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="179" data-end="650">Income inequality remains a persistent social and economic challenge in many countries, including the United States. Differences in earnings across demographic groups, particularly those based on <strong data-start="375" data-end="385">gender</strong> and <strong data-start="390" data-end="398">race</strong>, have significant implications for social mobility, economic policy, and workforce equity. Understanding the underlying factors that contribute to these disparities is critical for designing interventions that promote fairness and equal opportunity.</p>
<p data-start="652" data-end="1230">This study leverages the <strong data-start="677" data-end="701">Adult Census dataset</strong> from the UCI Machine Learning Repository, which contains demographic, educational, and occupational information for a large sample of U.S. adults, including variables such as age, education, occupation, hours worked per week, gender, race, and income. By analyzing this dataset, the study aims to quantify and explore patterns in wage differences across <strong data-start="1056" data-end="1066">gender</strong> and <strong data-start="1071" data-end="1088">racial groups</strong>, identify factors that contribute to these gaps, and provide insights that can inform discussions on equity and policy in the labor market.</p>
<p data-start="1232" data-end="1625">The focus of this research is both descriptive and analytical: first, to highlight observed disparities in income distribution, and second, to investigate the relationships between demographic characteristics and earning potential. The results are expected to illustrate how demographic attributes influence income outcomes and to highlight persistent wage gaps that warrant further attention.</p>

data-end="47">Data Source Description (with Citation)</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="49" data-end="565">The dataset used in this study is the <strong data-start="87" data-end="118">Adult Census Income dataset</strong>, obtained from the <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">UCI Machine Learning Repository</span></span>. This dataset is derived from the 1994 U.S. Census Bureau database and is commonly used for research in data mining and machine learning, particularly for classification and socioeconomic analysis tasks. It contains demographic and employment-related information for over 48,000 individuals, with the primary objective of predicting whether an individual’s annual income exceeds $50,000.</p>
<p data-start="567" data-end="1020">The dataset includes a range of attributes such as age, work class, education level, marital status, occupation, race, sex, hours worked per week, and native country. The target variable is a binary classification indicating whether an individual earns <strong data-start="820" data-end="845">≤50K or &gt;50K per year</strong>. These variables make the dataset suitable for analyzing income disparities across different demographic groups, including gender and race, which are central to this study.</p>
<p data-start="1022" data-end="1319">Prior to analysis, the dataset typically requires preprocessing steps such as handling missing values, encoding categorical variables, and removing inconsistencies. Despite these limitations, the dataset remains a widely accepted benchmark for exploring income-related patterns and inequalities.</p>

<hr data-start="357" data-end="360">
<h3 data-start="362" data-end="392"><span role="text"><strong data-start="366" data-end="392">Data Collection Method</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="394" data-end="772">The dataset used in this study originates from the 1994 United States Census database and is distributed through the <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">UCI Machine Learning Repository</span></span>. The data was collected by the <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">United States Census Bureau</span></span> using standardized survey methods designed to capture demographic, social, and economic information from a representative sample of the U.S. population.</p>
<p data-start="774" data-end="1138">Data collection was conducted through structured questionnaires administered to households, which included questions related to age, education, occupation, work class, income, and other personal attributes. The responses were self-reported by participants and later processed, anonymized, and organized into a structured format suitable for statistical analysis.</p>
<p data-start="1140" data-end="1454">The version of the dataset available in the repository is a cleaned and preprocessed subset of the original census data, where certain records with missing or inconsistent values may have been modified or removed. Additionally, categorical variables were encoded into standardized formats to facilitate analysis.</p>
<p data-start="1456" data-end="1812">While the dataset is widely used for research, it is important to note that it reflects data from a specific time period and relies on self-reported information, which may introduce biases or inaccuracies. Nevertheless, it remains a reliable source for examining general socioeconomic patterns, including income disparities across gender and racial groups.</p>

@w-lg/main:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col gap-4 grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="72fef0f5-6a88-4367-aaac-1097e3921504" dir="auto" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5-3" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-1"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word dark markdown-new-styling"><h3 data-start="0" data-end="37"><span role="text"><strong data-start="4" data-end="37">Data Cleaning and Preparation</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="39" data-end="279">Before conducting the analysis, the Adult Census dataset obtained from the <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">UCI Machine Learning Repository</span></span> was subjected to several data cleaning and preprocessing steps to ensure consistency, accuracy, and suitability for analysis.</p>
<p data-start="281" data-end="623">First, missing values were identified and handled. In this dataset, missing entries are commonly represented by the symbol “?”. Records containing missing values were either removed or appropriately treated to avoid introducing bias into the analysis. Duplicate records, if present, were also checked and removed to maintain data integrity.</p>
<p data-start="625" data-end="993">Next, categorical variables such as work class, education, marital status, occupation, race, sex, and native country were standardized and encoded into numerical formats to enable statistical analysis. This included converting text-based categories into consistent labels and, where necessary, applying encoding techniques such as label encoding or one-hot encoding.</p>
<p data-start="995" data-end="1410">Data transformation was also performed on selected variables. For instance, continuous variables such as age and hours worked per week were examined for outliers and inconsistencies. In some cases, grouping or binning was applied to simplify analysis and improve interpretability. Additionally, the income variable was retained as a binary classification (≤50K and &gt;50K) to align with the objectives of the study.</p>
<p data-start="1412" data-end="1705">Finally, the cleaned dataset was validated to ensure that all variables were in the correct format and that no critical information was lost during preprocessing. These steps ensured that the dataset was reliable and suitable for analyzing income disparities across gender and racial groups.</p>

<h3 data-start="159" data-end="198"><span role="text"><strong data-start="163" data-end="198">Where visuals are most valuable</strong></span></h3>
<ol data-start="200" data-end="867">
<li data-start="200" data-end="351">
<p data-start="203" data-end="236"><strong data-start="203" data-end="236">Data Cleaning and Preparation</strong></p>
<ul data-start="240" data-end="351">
<li data-start="240" data-end="291">
<p data-start="242" data-end="291">Missing values summary (e.g., bar chart or table)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="295" data-end="351">
<p data-start="297" data-end="351">Before/after cleaning comparison (optional but useful)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="353" data-end="552">
<p data-start="356" data-end="391"><strong data-start="356" data-end="391">Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)</strong></p>
<ul data-start="395" data-end="552">
<li data-start="395" data-end="431">
<p data-start="397" data-end="431">Income distribution (≤50K vs &gt;50K)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="435" data-end="465">
<p data-start="437" data-end="465">Gender vs income (bar chart)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="469" data-end="505">
<p data-start="471" data-end="505">Race vs income (stacked bar chart)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="509" data-end="552">
<p data-start="511" data-end="552">Boxplots (e.g., hours-per-week vs income)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="554" data-end="777">
<p data-start="557" data-end="591"><strong data-start="557" data-end="591">Key Analysis (Your Main Topic)</strong></p>
<ul data-start="595" data-end="777">
<li data-start="595" data-end="656">
<p data-start="597" data-end="656"><strong data-start="597" data-end="616">Gender wage gap</strong>: percentage of &gt;50K earners by gender</p>
</li>
<li data-start="660" data-end="725">
<p data-start="662" data-end="725"><strong data-start="662" data-end="681">Racial wage gap</strong>: income distribution across racial groups</p>
</li>
<li data-start="729" data-end="777">
<p data-start="731" data-end="777">Cross-analysis (e.g., gender + race vs income)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="779" data-end="867">
<p data-start="782" data-end="815"><strong data-start="782" data-end="815">Model Results (if applicable)</strong></p>
<ul data-start="819" data-end="867">
<li data-start="819" data-end="837">
<p data-start="821" data-end="837">Confusion matrix</p>
</li>
<li data-start="841" data-end="867">
<p data-start="843" data-end="867">Feature importance chart</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="869" data-end="872">
<h3 data-start="874" data-end="896"><span role="text"><strong data-start="878" data-end="896">Best practices</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="897" data-end="1223">
<li data-start="897" data-end="1063">
<p data-start="899" data-end="921">Each figure must have:</p>
<ul data-start="924" data-end="1063">
<li data-start="924" data-end="962">
<p data-start="926" data-end="962">A <strong data-start="928" data-end="945">figure number</strong> (e.g., Figure 1)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="965" data-end="984">
<p data-start="967" data-end="984">A <strong data-start="969" data-end="984">clear title</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="987" data-end="1063">
<p data-start="989" data-end="1063">A <strong data-start="991" data-end="1024">short explanation in the text</strong> (interpretation, not just description)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1065" data-end="1223">
<p data-start="1067" data-end="1080">Keep visuals:</p>
<ul data-start="1083" data-end="1223">
<li data-start="1083" data-end="1106">
<p data-start="1085" data-end="1106">Simple and readable</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1109" data-end="1164">
<p data-start="1111" data-end="1164">Directly tied to your research question (wage gaps)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1167" data-end="1223">
<p data-start="1169" data-end="1223">Limited to the most important insights (avoid clutter)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1225" data-end="1228">
<h3 data-start="1230" data-end="1266"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1234" data-end="1266">When NOT to include pictures</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="1267" data-end="1388">
<li data-start="1267" data-end="1329">
<p data-start="1269" data-end="1329">If they repeat information already clear in text or tables</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1330" data-end="1388">
<p data-start="1332" data-end="1388">If they are not discussed or interpreted in your paper</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1390" data-end="1393">
<h3 data-start="1395" data-end="1434"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1399" data-end="1434">Recommendation for your project</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="1435" data-end="1479">Include around <strong data-start="1450" data-end="1469">4–6 key visuals</strong>, such as:</p>
<ul data-start="1480" data-end="1617">
<li data-start="1480" data-end="1503">
<p data-start="1482" data-end="1503">Income distribution</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1504" data-end="1524">
<p data-start="1506" data-end="1524">Gender vs income</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1525" data-end="1543">
<p data-start="1527" data-end="1543">Race vs income</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1544" data-end="1579">
<p data-start="1546" data-end="1579">Combined gender–race comparison</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1580" data-end="1617">
<p data-start="1582" data-end="1617">(Optional) hours worked vs income</p>
</li>
</ul>

flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word dark markdown-new-styling"><h3 data-start="0" data-end="29"><span role="text"><strong data-start="4" data-end="29">Dashboard Explanation</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="31" data-end="458">The dashboard developed for this study provides an interactive visualization of income distribution and demographic patterns using the Adult Census dataset from the <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">UCI Machine Learning Repository</span></span>. Its primary objective is to present key insights related to <strong data-start="295" data-end="333">gender and racial wage disparities</strong> in a clear and accessible format, enabling users to explore relationships between demographic variables and income levels.</p>
<p data-start="460" data-end="935">The dashboard integrates multiple visual components to summarize the dataset. These include bar charts illustrating income distribution across gender and racial groups, as well as comparative visualizations showing the proportion of individuals earning above and below $50,000 annually. Additional charts display relationships between income and other relevant variables such as education level and hours worked per week, providing further context for observed disparities.</p>
<p data-start="937" data-end="1296">Interactive features are incorporated to enhance usability. Users can apply filters based on attributes such as gender, race, education level, or work class, allowing for more focused analysis of specific subgroups. These features enable dynamic exploration of the dataset and support deeper understanding of how different factors influence income outcomes.</p>
<p data-start="1298" data-end="1737">The dashboard is designed to highlight key patterns and trends, such as differences in income distribution between demographic groups and the potential impact of socioeconomic factors. However, it is important to note that the insights presented are based on historical data and may not fully reflect current conditions. Additionally, as the dataset relies on self-reported census information, some limitations and biases may be present.</p>
<p data-start="1739" data-end="1958">Overall, the dashboard serves as a visual analytical tool that complements the statistical findings of the study, making complex data more interpretable and supporting informed conclusions regarding income inequality.</p>

<h3 data-start="1307" data-end="1368"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1311" data-end="1349">6. Key Insights and Interpretation</strong> ⭐ (MOST IMPORTANT)</span></h3>
<p data-start="1369" data-end="1423"><strong data-start="1369" data-end="1388">Include images?</strong> ✅✅ Yes (3–5 visuals recommended)</p>

</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1674" data-end="1688"><strong data-start="1674" data-end="1688">Placement:</strong></p>
<ul data-start="1689" data-end="1827">
<li data-start="1689" data-end="1773">
<p data-start="1691" data-end="1773">Each visual should appear <strong data-start="1717" data-end="1773">right before or after the paragraph that explains it</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1774" data-end="1827">
<p data-start="1776" data-end="1827">Always interpret the chart (do not just display it)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1829" data-end="1832">
<h3 data-start="1834" data-end="1864"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1838" data-end="1864">7. Dataset Limitations</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="1865" data-end="1891"><strong data-start="1865" data-end="1884">Include images?</strong> ❌ No</p>
<ul data-start="1892" data-end="1927">
<li data-start="1892" data-end="1927">
<p data-start="1894" data-end="1927">Keep this concise and text-based.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1929" data-end="1932">
<h3 data-start="1934" data-end="1975"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1938" data-end="1975">8. Conclusion and Recommendations</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="1976" data-end="2002"><strong data-start="1976" data-end="1995">Include images?</strong> ❌ No</p>
<ul data-start="2003" data-end="2054">
<li data-start="2003" data-end="2054">
<p data-start="2005" data-end="2054">Summarize findings; visuals are unnecessary here.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2056" data-end="2059">
<h2 data-start="2061" data-end="2093"><span role="text"><strong data-start="2064" data-end="2093">Summary (Quick Reference)</strong></span></h2>
<div class="TyagGW_tableContainer"><div tabindex="-1" class="group TyagGW_tableWrapper flex flex-col-reverse w-fit"><table data-start="2095" data-end="2737" class="w-fit min-w-(--thread-content-width)"><thead data-start="2095" data-end="2160"><tr data-start="2095" data-end="2160"><th data-start="2095" data-end="2130" data-col-size="sm" class="">Section</th><th data-start="2130" data-end="2148" data-col-size="sm" class="">Include Images?</th><th data-start="2148" data-end="2160" data-col-size="sm" class="">How Many</th></tr></thead><tbody data-start="2226" data-end="2737"><tr data-start="2226" data-end="2289"><td data-start="2226" data-end="2261" data-col-size="sm">Introduction</td><td data-start="2261" data-end="2277" data-col-size="sm">❌ No</td><td data-start="2277" data-end="2289" data-col-size="sm">0</td></tr><tr data-start="2290" data-end="2354"><td data-start="2290" data-end="2325" data-col-size="sm">Data Source Description</td><td data-start="2325" data-end="2342" data-col-size="sm">⚠️ Optional</td><td data-start="2342" data-end="2354" data-col-size="sm">0–1</td></tr><tr data-start="2355" data-end="2418"><td data-start="2355" data-end="2390" data-col-size="sm">Data Collection Method</td><td data-start="2390" data-end="2406" data-col-size="sm">❌ No</td><td data-start="2406" data-end="2418" data-col-size="sm">0</td></tr><tr data-start="2419" data-end="2482"><td data-start="2419" data-end="2454" data-col-size="sm">Data Cleaning &amp; Preparation</td><td data-start="2454" data-end="2470" data-col-size="sm">✅ Yes</td><td data-start="2470" data-end="2482" data-col-size="sm">1–2</td></tr><tr data-start="2483" data-end="2546"><td data-start="2483" data-end="2518" data-col-size="sm">Dashboard Explanation</td><td data-start="2518" data-end="2534" data-col-size="sm">✅ Yes</td><td data-start="2534" data-end="2546" data-col-size="sm">1</td></tr><tr data-start="2547" data-end="2609"><td data-start="2547" data-end="2582" data-col-size="sm">Key Insights &amp; Recommendations</td><td data-start="2709" data-end="2725" data-col-size="sm">❌ No</td><td data-start="2725" data-end="2737" data-col-size="sm">0</td></tr></tbody></table><div class="sticky h-0 select-none end-(--thread-content-margin) self-end"><div class="absolute end-0 flex items-end" style="height: 32.7969px;"><span class="" data-state="closed"><button aria-label="Copy table" class="hover:bg-token-bg-tertiary text-token-text-secondary my-1 rounded-sm p-1 transition-opacity group-[:not(:hover):not(:focus-within)]:pointer-events-none group-[:not(:hover):not(:focus-within)]:opacity-0"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true" class="icon"><use href="/cdn/assets/sprites-core-cohbnghc.svg#ce3544" fill="currentColor"></use></svg></button></span></div></div></div></div>
<hr data-start="2739" data-end="2742">
<h2 data-start="2744" data-end="2765"><span role="text"><strong data-start="2747" data-end="2765">Important Rule</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="2766" data-end="2783">Every image must:</p>
<ol data-start="2784" data-end="2911">
<li data-start="2784" data-end="2828">
<p data-start="2787" data-end="2828">Be labeled (e.g., <em data-start="2805" data-end="2825">Figure 1, Figure 2</em>)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2829" data-end="2846">
<p data-start="2832" data-end="2846">Have a title</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2847" data-end="2911">
<p data-start="2850" data-end="2911">Be explained in the text (<strong data-start="2876" data-end="2908">this is critical for grading</strong>)</p>
</li>
</ol>

charts.</p>
<hr data-start="144" data-end="147">
<h2 data-start="149" data-end="183"><span role="text"><strong data-start="152" data-end="183">What “Full Dashboard” Means</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="184" data-end="236">A <strong data-start="186" data-end="204">full dashboard</strong> is a single view that contains:</p>
<ul data-start="237" data-end="307">
<li data-start="237" data-end="262">
<p data-start="239" data-end="262">Multiple charts/visuals</p>
</li>
<li data-start="263" data-end="284">
<p data-start="265" data-end="284">Filters or controls</p>
</li>
<li data-start="285" data-end="307">
<p data-start="287" data-end="307">Key metrics (if any)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="309" data-end="413">It shows <strong data-start="318" data-end="362">how all components are arranged together</strong>, giving a complete overview of your analysis tool.</p>
<hr data-start="415" data-end="418">
<h2 data-start="420" data-end="478"><span role="text"><strong data-start="423" data-end="478">What Your Dashboard Should Contain (for your topic)</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="480" data-end="562">Since your focus is <strong data-start="500" data-end="530">gender and racial wage gap</strong>, your dashboard should include:</p>
<h3 data-start="564" data-end="604"><span role="text"><strong data-start="568" data-end="604">Core Visuals (inside one screen)</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="605" data-end="922">
<li data-start="605" data-end="672">
<p data-start="607" data-end="630"><strong data-start="607" data-end="630">Income Distribution</strong></p>
<ul data-start="633" data-end="672">
<li data-start="633" data-end="672">
<p data-start="635" data-end="672">Pie chart or bar chart (≤50K vs &gt;50K)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="674" data-end="758">
<p data-start="676" data-end="696"><strong data-start="676" data-end="696">Gender vs Income</strong></p>
<ul data-start="699" data-end="758">
<li data-start="699" data-end="758">
<p data-start="701" data-end="758">Bar chart showing proportion of high/low income by gender</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="760" data-end="839">
<p data-start="762" data-end="780"><strong data-start="762" data-end="780">Race vs Income</strong></p>
<ul data-start="783" data-end="839">
<li data-start="783" data-end="839">
<p data-start="785" data-end="839">Stacked bar chart comparing income levels across races</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="841" data-end="922">
<p data-start="843" data-end="872"><strong data-start="843" data-end="872">Optional Supporting Chart</strong></p>
<ul data-start="875" data-end="922">
<li data-start="875" data-end="922">
<p data-start="877" data-end="922">Hours worked vs income OR education vs income</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="924" data-end="927">
<h3 data-start="929" data-end="955"><span role="text"><strong data-start="933" data-end="955">Filters / Controls</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="956" data-end="987">Place these on the side or top:</p>
<ul data-start="988" data-end="1042">
<li data-start="988" data-end="998">
<p data-start="990" data-end="998">Gender</p>
</li>
<li data-start="999" data-end="1007">
<p data-start="1001" data-end="1007">Race</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1008" data-end="1027">
<p data-start="1010" data-end="1027">Education level</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1028" data-end="1042">
<p data-start="1030" data-end="1042">Work class</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1044" data-end="1126">These allow interaction (even if you don’t demonstrate it, include them visually).</p>
<hr data-start="1128" data-end="1131">
<h3 data-start="1133" data-end="1190"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1137" data-end="1190">Layout Example (What your screenshot should show)</strong></span></h3>
<pre class="overflow-visible! px-0!" data-start="1192" data-end="1437"><div class="relative w-full mt-4 mb-1"><div class=""><div class="relative"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0" style="width: 100%; transform: none;"><div class="border border-token-border-light border-radius-3xl corner-superellipse/1.1 rounded-3xl"><div class="h-full w-full border-radius-3xl bg-token-bg-elevated-secondary corner-superellipse/1.1 overflow-clip rounded-3xl lxnfua_clipPathFallback"><div class="pointer-events-none absolute end-1.5 top-1 z-2 md:end-2 md:top-1"><button class="flex gap-1 items-center select-none pointer-events-auto py-2 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-black/5 dark:hover:bg-white/10 size-9 rounded-full px-2" aria-label="Copy" data-state="closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true" class="icon-md"><use href="/cdn/assets/sprites-core-cohbnghc.svg#ce3544" fill="currentColor"></use></svg></button></div><div class="pe-11 pt-3" style="transform: none;"><div class="relative z-0 flex max-w-full"><div id="code-block-viewer" dir="ltr" class="q9tKkq_viewer cm-editor z-10 light:cm-light dark:cm-light flex h-full w-full flex-col items-stretch ͼk ͼy"><div class="cm-scroller"><div class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly"><span>[ Title: Income Inequality Dashboard ]</span><br><br><span>[ Filters: Gender | Race | Education | Work Class ]</span><br><br><span>[ Chart 1: Income Distribution ]</span><br><span>[ Chart 2: Gender vs Income ]</span><br><br><span>[ Chart 3: Race vs Income ]</span><br><span>[ Chart 4: (Optional) Education or Hours vs Income ]</span></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class=""><div class="" style="width: 100%; transform: none;"></div></div></div></div></div></pre>
<hr data-start="1439" data-end="1442">
<h2 data-start="1444" data-end="1476"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1447" data-end="1476">What Screenshot to Insert</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="1477" data-end="1512">Take <strong data-start="1482" data-end="1500">one screenshot</strong> that shows:</p>
<ul data-start="1513" data-end="1585">
<li data-start="1513" data-end="1546">
<p data-start="1515" data-end="1546">The <strong data-start="1519" data-end="1546">entire dashboard window</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1547" data-end="1567">
<p data-start="1549" data-end="1567">All charts visible</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1568" data-end="1585">
<p data-start="1570" data-end="1585">Filters visible</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1587" data-end="1597">📌 Do NOT:</p>
<ul data-start="1598" data-end="1703">
<li data-start="1598" data-end="1643">
<p data-start="1600" data-end="1643">Insert separate images of each chart here</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1644" data-end="1703">
<p data-start="1646" data-end="1703">Crop too tightly (it should look like a system/interface)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1705" data-end="1708">
<h2 data-start="1710" data-end="1749"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1713" data-end="1749">How to Refer to It in Your Paper</strong></span></h2>
<ul data-start="1750" data-end="2011">
<li data-start="1750" data-end="1843">
<p data-start="1752" data-end="1843">Label:<br data-start="1758" data-end="1761">
<strong data-start="1763" data-end="1843">Figure X: Dashboard Overview of Income Distribution and Demographic Analysis</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1845" data-end="2011">
<p data-start="1847" data-end="2011">In text:<br data-start="1855" data-end="1858">
“As shown in Figure X, the dashboard presents multiple visualizations that allow users to explore income distribution across gender and racial groups…”</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2013" data-end="2016">
<h2 data-start="2018" data-end="2042"><span role="text"><strong data-start="2021" data-end="2042">Tools You Can Use</strong></span></h2>
<ul data-start="2043" data-end="2183">
<li data-start="2043" data-end="2081">
<p data-start="2045" data-end="2081">Excel (basic dashboard with slicers)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2082" data-end="2125">
<p data-start="2084" data-end="2125">Power BI (recommended for cleaner layout)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2126" data-end="2135">
<p data-start="2128" data-end="2135">Tableau</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2136" data-end="2183">
<p data-start="2138" data-end="2183">Python (Plotly Dash / Streamlit, if advanced)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2185" data-end="2188">
<h2 data-start="2190" data-end="2205"><span role="text"><strong data-start="2193" data-end="2205">Key Idea</strong></span></h2>
<ul data-start="2206" data-end="2327">
<li data-start="2206" data-end="2271">
<p data-start="2208" data-end="2271"><strong data-start="2208" data-end="2271">Dashboard Explanation section = 1 full interface screenshot</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="2272" data-end="2327">
<p data-start="2274" data-end="2327"><strong data-start="2274" data-end="2327">Key Insights section = individual charts analyzed</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2329" data-end="2332">

flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="253">Using <strong data-start="6" data-end="47"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Google Colab</span></span></strong> is a good choice for development, but <strong data-start="86" data-end="145">Colab alone does NOT produce a “working dashboard link”</strong> in the way instructors usually expect. Colab is primarily for code execution, not hosting interactive apps.</p>
<p data-start="255" data-end="387">To meet the requirement of a <strong data-start="284" data-end="310">working dashboard link</strong>, you should use Colab <strong data-start="333" data-end="372">together with a dashboard framework</strong> and deploy it.</p>
<hr data-start="389" data-end="392">
<h1 data-start="394" data-end="441"><span role="text"><strong data-start="396" data-end="441">Recommended Approach (Best for Your Case)</strong></span></h1>
<h2 data-start="443" data-end="492"><span role="text"><strong data-start="446" data-end="492">Option 1: Streamlit (Strongly Recommended)</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="493" data-end="570">Use Colab to build, then deploy via <strong data-start="529" data-end="570"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Streamlit</span></span></strong></p>
<h3 data-start="572" data-end="593"><span role="text"><strong data-start="576" data-end="593">Why Streamlit</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="594" data-end="715">
<li data-start="594" data-end="628">
<p data-start="596" data-end="628">Simple Python-based dashboards</p>
</li>
<li data-start="629" data-end="676">
<p data-start="631" data-end="676">Built-in interactivity (filters, dropdowns)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="677" data-end="715">
<p data-start="679" data-end="715">Easy to deploy and share as a link</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="717" data-end="733"><span role="text"><strong data-start="721" data-end="733">Workflow</strong></span></h3>
<ol data-start="734" data-end="1013">
<li data-start="734" data-end="857">
<p data-start="737" data-end="750"><strong data-start="737" data-end="750">In Colab:</strong></p>
<ul data-start="754" data-end="857">
<li data-start="754" data-end="787">
<p data-start="756" data-end="787">Load and clean dataset (pandas)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="791" data-end="827">
<p data-start="793" data-end="827">Build charts (matplotlib / plotly)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="831" data-end="857">
<p data-start="833" data-end="857">Write Streamlit app code</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="859" data-end="894">
<p data-start="862" data-end="894"><strong data-start="862" data-end="894">Move code to local or GitHub</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="896" data-end="1013">
<p data-start="899" data-end="916"><strong data-start="899" data-end="916">Deploy using:</strong></p>
<ul data-start="920" data-end="1013">
<li data-start="920" data-end="961">
<p data-start="922" data-end="961"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Streamlit Community Cloud</span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="965" data-end="1013">
<p data-start="967" data-end="1013">You get a <strong data-start="977" data-end="1013">public URL (your dashboard link)</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="1015" data-end="1018">
<h2 data-start="1020" data-end="1048"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1023" data-end="1048">Option 2: Plotly Dash</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="1049" data-end="1094">Use <strong data-start="1053" data-end="1094"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Plotly Dash</span></span></strong></p>
<ul data-start="1096" data-end="1207">
<li data-start="1096" data-end="1132">
<p data-start="1098" data-end="1132">More customizable than Streamlit</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1133" data-end="1158">
<p data-start="1135" data-end="1158">Slightly more complex</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1159" data-end="1207">
<p data-start="1161" data-end="1207">Also deployable online (Render, Railway, etc.)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1209" data-end="1212">
<h2 data-start="1214" data-end="1274"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1217" data-end="1274">Option 3: Google Colab + ngrok (Quick but Less Ideal)</strong></span></h2>
<ul data-start="1275" data-end="1416">
<li data-start="1275" data-end="1375">
<p data-start="1277" data-end="1375">You can run a dashboard inside Colab and expose it using <strong data-start="1334" data-end="1375"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">ngrok</span></span></strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1376" data-end="1416">
<p data-start="1378" data-end="1416">This generates a temporary public link</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1418" data-end="1431">⚠️ Downsides:</p>
<ul data-start="1432" data-end="1510">
<li data-start="1432" data-end="1466">
<p data-start="1434" data-end="1466">Link expires when session ends</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1467" data-end="1510">
<p data-start="1469" data-end="1510">Not ideal for submission unless allowed</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1512" data-end="1515">
<h1 data-start="1517" data-end="1571"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1519" data-end="1571">What Your Dashboard Should Include (for grading)</strong></span></h1>
<p data-start="1573" data-end="1592">Regardless of tool:</p>
<h3 data-start="1594" data-end="1621"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1598" data-end="1621">Required Components</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="1622" data-end="1834">
<li data-start="1622" data-end="1660">
<p data-start="1624" data-end="1660">Title: <em data-start="1631" data-end="1660">Income Inequality Dashboard</em></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1661" data-end="1716">
<p data-start="1663" data-end="1671">Filters:</p>
<ul data-start="1674" data-end="1716">
<li data-start="1674" data-end="1682">
<p data-start="1676" data-end="1682">Gender</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1685" data-end="1691">
<p data-start="1687" data-end="1691">Race</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1694" data-end="1716">
<p data-start="1696" data-end="1716">Education (optional)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1717" data-end="1834">
<p data-start="1719" data-end="1726">Charts:</p>
<ul data-start="1729" data-end="1834">
<li data-start="1729" data-end="1750">
<p data-start="1731" data-end="1750">Income distribution</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1753" data-end="1771">
<p data-start="1755" data-end="1771">Gender vs income</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1774" data-end="1790">
<p data-start="1776" data-end="1790">Race vs income</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1793" data-end="1834">
<p data-start="1795" data-end="1834">(Optional) Education or hours vs income</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1836" data-end="1839">
<h1 data-start="1841" data-end="1862"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1843" data-end="1862">What You Submit</strong></span></h1>
<ol data-start="1863" data-end="2023">
<li data-start="1863" data-end="1913">
<p data-start="1866" data-end="1913"><strong data-start="1866" data-end="1884">Dashboard Link</strong> (Streamlit / deployed app)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1914" data-end="1977">
<p data-start="1917" data-end="1977"><strong data-start="1917" data-end="1931">Screenshot</strong> → goes into <em data-start="1944" data-end="1975">Dashboard Explanation section</em></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1978" data-end="2023">
<p data-start="1981" data-end="2023"><strong data-start="1981" data-end="2000">Code (optional)</strong> → appendix or GitHub</p>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="2025" data-end="2028">
<h1 data-start="2030" data-end="2050"><span role="text"><strong data-start="2032" data-end="2050">Recommendation</strong></span></h1>
<p data-start="2051" data-end="2066">Use this stack:</p>
<ul data-start="2067" data-end="2165">
<li data-start="2067" data-end="2106">
<p data-start="2069" data-end="2106">Colab → data cleaning + prototyping</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2107" data-end="2132">
<p data-start="2109" data-end="2132">Streamlit → dashboard</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2133" data-end="2165">
<p data-start="2135" data-end="2165">Streamlit Cloud → deployment</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2167" data-end="2254">This is the <strong data-start="2179" data-end="2253">simplest way to meet the “working dashboard link” requirement reliably</strong>.</p>

</li>
<li data-start="2357" data-end="2431" data-is-last-node="">

data-end="78">data cleaning can be done using <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Microsoft Excel</span></span></strong>, and it is acceptable for many academic projects—especially if the dataset is not extremely large (the Adult Census dataset is manageable).</p>
<p data-start="220" data-end="292">However, there are <strong data-start="239" data-end="268">strengths and limitations</strong> you should be aware of.</p>
<hr data-start="294" data-end="297">
<h1 data-start="299" data-end="349"><span role="text"><strong data-start="301" data-end="349">What You CAN Do in Excel (Recommended Tasks)</strong></span></h1>
<h3 data-start="351" data-end="383"><span role="text"><strong data-start="355" data-end="383">1. Handle Missing Values</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="384" data-end="545">
<li data-start="384" data-end="501">
<p data-start="386" data-end="427">Use <strong data-start="390" data-end="408">Find &amp; Replace</strong> to locate “?” and:</p>
<ul data-start="430" data-end="501">
<li data-start="430" data-end="456">
<p data-start="432" data-end="456">Replace with blank, or</p>
</li>
<li data-start="459" data-end="501">
<p data-start="461" data-end="501">Replace with a category like “Unknown”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="502" data-end="545">
<p data-start="504" data-end="545">Or filter rows and delete missing entries</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="547" data-end="550">
<h3 data-start="552" data-end="580"><span role="text"><strong data-start="556" data-end="580">2. Remove Duplicates</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="581" data-end="620">
<li data-start="581" data-end="620">
<p data-start="583" data-end="620">Use:<br data-start="587" data-end="590">
<strong data-start="592" data-end="620">Data → Remove Duplicates</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="622" data-end="625">
<h3 data-start="627" data-end="654"><span role="text"><strong data-start="631" data-end="654">3. Standardize Data</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="655" data-end="814">
<li data-start="655" data-end="758">
<p data-start="657" data-end="681">Clean inconsistent text:</p>
<ul data-start="684" data-end="758">
<li data-start="684" data-end="706">
<p data-start="686" data-end="706">Trim spaces (<code data-start="699" data-end="705">TRIM</code>)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="709" data-end="758">
<p data-start="711" data-end="758">Fix capitalization (<code data-start="731" data-end="738">UPPER</code>, <code data-start="740" data-end="747">LOWER</code>, <code data-start="749" data-end="757">PROPER</code>)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="759" data-end="814">
<p data-start="761" data-end="814">Ensure categories like “Male” / “male” are consistent</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="816" data-end="819">
<h3 data-start="821" data-end="859"><span role="text"><strong data-start="825" data-end="859">4. Basic Filtering and Sorting</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="860" data-end="940">
<li data-start="860" data-end="900">
<p data-start="862" data-end="900">Filter by columns (e.g., race, gender)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="901" data-end="940">
<p data-start="903" data-end="940">Check for anomalies or unusual values</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="942" data-end="945">
<h3 data-start="947" data-end="976"><span role="text"><strong data-start="951" data-end="976">5. Create New Columns</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="977" data-end="1085">
<li data-start="977" data-end="1085">
<p data-start="979" data-end="987">Example:</p>
<ul data-start="990" data-end="1085">
<li data-start="990" data-end="1037">
<p data-start="992" data-end="1037">Convert income into binary labels (if needed)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1040" data-end="1085">
<p data-start="1042" data-end="1085">Group ages into ranges (e.g., 20–30, 30–40)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1087" data-end="1090">
<h3 data-start="1092" data-end="1133"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1096" data-end="1133">6. Basic Visualization (Optional)</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="1134" data-end="1203">
<li data-start="1134" data-end="1150">
<p data-start="1136" data-end="1150">Pivot tables</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1151" data-end="1203">
<p data-start="1153" data-end="1203">Bar charts (for quick validation before dashboard)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1205" data-end="1208">
<h1 data-start="1210" data-end="1265"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1212" data-end="1265">Limitations of Excel (Important for Your Project)</strong></span></h1>
<h3 data-start="1267" data-end="1308"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1271" data-end="1308">1. Encoding Categorical Variables</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="1309" data-end="1406">
<li data-start="1309" data-end="1406">
<p data-start="1311" data-end="1341">Excel does not easily support:</p>
<ul data-start="1344" data-end="1406">
<li data-start="1344" data-end="1364">
<p data-start="1346" data-end="1364">One-hot encoding</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1367" data-end="1406">
<p data-start="1369" data-end="1406">Label encoding for machine learning</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="1408" data-end="1434"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1412" data-end="1434">2. Reproducibility</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="1435" data-end="1493">
<li data-start="1435" data-end="1493">
<p data-start="1437" data-end="1493">Harder to document exact steps compared to code (Python)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="1495" data-end="1517"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1499" data-end="1517">3. Scalability</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="1518" data-end="1583">
<li data-start="1518" data-end="1583">
<p data-start="1520" data-end="1583">Works fine for ~50k rows, but not ideal for very large datasets</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1585" data-end="1588">
<h1 data-start="1590" data-end="1632"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1592" data-end="1632">Best Practice (Recommended Workflow)</strong></span></h1>
<p data-start="1634" data-end="1660">Use a <strong data-start="1640" data-end="1659">hybrid approach</strong>:</p>
<ol data-start="1662" data-end="1883">
<li data-start="1662" data-end="1737">
<p data-start="1665" data-end="1674"><strong data-start="1665" data-end="1674">Excel</strong></p>
<ul data-start="1678" data-end="1737">
<li data-start="1678" data-end="1737">
<p data-start="1680" data-end="1737">Initial cleaning (missing values, duplicates, formatting)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1739" data-end="1883">
<p data-start="1742" data-end="1791"><strong data-start="1742" data-end="1791">Then in <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Google Colab</span></span></strong></p>
<ul data-start="1795" data-end="1883">
<li data-start="1795" data-end="1846">
<p data-start="1797" data-end="1846">Further preprocessing (encoding, transformations)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1850" data-end="1883">
<p data-start="1852" data-end="1883">Analysis and dashboard creation</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="1885" data-end="1888">
<h1 data-start="1890" data-end="1925"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1892" data-end="1925">How to Write It in Your Paper</strong></span></h1>
<p data-start="1927" data-end="1941">You can state:</p>
<blockquote data-start="1943" data-end="2183">
<p data-start="1945" data-end="2183">“Initial data cleaning, including handling missing values, removing duplicates, and standardizing categorical variables, was performed using Microsoft Excel. Further preprocessing and analysis were conducted using Python in Google Colab.”</p>
</blockquote>
<hr data-start="2185" data-end="2188">
<h1 data-start="2190" data-end="2210"><span role="text"><strong data-start="2192" data-end="2210">Recommendation</strong></span></h1>
<ul data-start="2211" data-end="2350">
<li data-start="2211" data-end="2257">
<p data-start="2213" data-end="2257">Yes, Excel is <strong data-start="2227" data-end="2255">acceptable and practical</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="2258" data-end="2350">
<p data-start="2260" data-end="2350">But for your <strong data-start="2273" data-end="2298">dashboard requirement</strong>, you will still need Python tools (e.g., Streamlit)</p>
</li>
</ul>

data-end="15">dashboard</strong> is a <strong data-start="21" data-end="114">visual interface that displays key data, metrics, and insights in a single organized view</strong>, allowing users to quickly monitor, analyze, and make decisions.</p>
<hr data-start="181" data-end="184">
<h2 data-start="186" data-end="202"><span role="text"><strong data-start="189" data-end="202">Core Idea</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="203" data-end="370">A dashboard consolidates data into <strong data-start="238" data-end="272">charts, graphs, and indicators</strong> so that important information can be understood at a glance, rather than reading raw data tables.</p>
<hr data-start="372" data-end="375">
<h2 data-start="377" data-end="403"><span role="text"><strong data-start="380" data-end="403">Key Characteristics</strong></span></h2>
<h3 data-start="405" data-end="439"><span role="text"><strong data-start="409" data-end="439">1. Multiple Visualizations</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="440" data-end="545">
<li data-start="440" data-end="481">
<p data-start="442" data-end="481">Combines several charts in one screen</p>
</li>
<li data-start="482" data-end="545">
<p data-start="484" data-end="493">Examples:</p>
<ul data-start="496" data-end="545">
<li data-start="496" data-end="510">
<p data-start="498" data-end="510">Bar charts</p>
</li>
<li data-start="513" data-end="527">
<p data-start="515" data-end="527">Pie charts</p>
</li>
<li data-start="530" data-end="545">
<p data-start="532" data-end="545">Line graphs</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="547" data-end="550">
<h3 data-start="552" data-end="590"><span role="text"><strong data-start="556" data-end="590">2. Focus on Key Metrics (KPIs)</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="591" data-end="760">
<li data-start="591" data-end="639">
<p data-start="593" data-end="639">Displays the most important information only</p>
</li>
<li data-start="640" data-end="760">
<p data-start="642" data-end="669">Example (for your project):</p>
<ul data-start="672" data-end="760">
<li data-start="672" data-end="690">
<p data-start="674" data-end="690">% earning &gt;50K</p>
</li>
<li data-start="693" data-end="726">
<p data-start="695" data-end="726">Income distribution by gender</p>
</li>
<li data-start="729" data-end="760">
<p data-start="731" data-end="760">Income distribution by race</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="762" data-end="765">
<h3 data-start="767" data-end="807"><span role="text"><strong data-start="771" data-end="807">3. Interactivity (in most cases)</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="808" data-end="918">
<li data-start="808" data-end="918">
<p data-start="810" data-end="820">Users can:</p>
<ul data-start="823" data-end="918">
<li data-start="823" data-end="864">
<p data-start="825" data-end="864">Filter data (e.g., by gender or race)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="867" data-end="888">
<p data-start="869" data-end="888">Select categories</p>
</li>
<li data-start="891" data-end="918">
<p data-start="893" data-end="918">Drill down into details</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="920" data-end="923">
<h3 data-start="925" data-end="954"><span role="text"><strong data-start="929" data-end="954">4. Single View Layout</strong></span></h3>
<ul data-start="955" data-end="1042">
<li data-start="955" data-end="1007">
<p data-start="957" data-end="1007">Everything is arranged in <strong data-start="983" data-end="1005">one screen or page</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1008" data-end="1042">
<p data-start="1010" data-end="1042">Designed for quick understanding</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1044" data-end="1047">
<h2 data-start="1049" data-end="1086"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1052" data-end="1086">Example (Your Project Context)</strong></span></h2>
<p data-start="1088" data-end="1117">Your dashboard might include:</p>
<ul data-start="1118" data-end="1320">
<li data-start="1118" data-end="1167">
<p data-start="1120" data-end="1167">A chart showing <strong data-start="1136" data-end="1167">overall income distribution</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1168" data-end="1213">
<p data-start="1170" data-end="1213">A chart comparing <strong data-start="1188" data-end="1213">male vs female income</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1214" data-end="1265">
<p data-start="1216" data-end="1265">A chart comparing <strong data-start="1234" data-end="1265">income across racial groups</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1266" data-end="1320">
<p data-start="1268" data-end="1280">Filters for:</p>
<ul data-start="1283" data-end="1320">
<li data-start="1283" data-end="1293">
<p data-start="1285" data-end="1293">Gender</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1296" data-end="1304">
<p data-start="1298" data-end="1304">Race</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1307" data-end="1320">
<p data-start="1309" data-end="1320">Education</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1322" data-end="1368">All of these appear together in one interface.</p>
<hr data-start="1370" data-end="1373">
<h2 data-start="1375" data-end="1402"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1378" data-end="1402">Dashboard vs. Charts</strong></span></h2>
<div class="TyagGW_tableContainer"><div tabindex="-1" class="group TyagGW_tableWrapper flex flex-col-reverse w-fit"><table data-start="1404" data-end="1896" class="w-fit min-w-(--thread-content-width)"><thead data-start="1404" data-end="1486"><tr data-start="1404" data-end="1486"><th data-start="1404" data-end="1420" data-col-size="sm" class="">Aspect</th><th data-start="1420" data-end="1448" data-col-size="sm" class="">Chart</th><th data-start="1448" data-end="1486" data-col-size="sm" class="">Dashboard</th></tr></thead><tbody data-start="1569" data-end="1896"><tr data-start="1569" data-end="1650"><td data-start="1569" data-end="1585" data-col-size="sm">Scope</td><td data-start="1585" data-end="1612" data-col-size="sm">Single visualization</td><td data-start="1612" data-end="1650" data-col-size="sm">Multiple visualizations</td></tr><tr data-start="1651" data-end="1732"><td data-start="1651" data-end="1667" data-col-size="sm">Purpose</td><td data-start="1667" data-end="1694" data-col-size="sm">Show one relationship</td><td data-start="1694" data-end="1732" data-col-size="sm">Provide overall insight</td></tr><tr data-start="1733" data-end="1814"><td data-start="1733" data-end="1749" data-col-size="sm">Interaction</td><td data-start="1749" data-end="1776" data-col-size="sm">Limited</td><td data-start="1776" data-end="1814" data-col-size="sm">Often interactive</td></tr><tr data-start="1815" data-end="1896"><td data-start="1815" data-end="1831" data-col-size="sm">Layout</td><td data-start="1831" data-end="1858" data-col-size="sm">Standalone</td><td data-start="1858" data-end="1896" data-col-size="sm">Integrated in one screen</td></tr></tbody></table><div class="sticky h-0 select-none end-(--thread-content-margin) self-end"><div class="absolute end-0 flex items-end" style="height: 32.7969px;"><span class="" data-state="closed"><button aria-label="Copy table" class="hover:bg-token-bg-tertiary text-token-text-secondary my-1 rounded-sm p-1 transition-opacity group-[:not(:hover):not(:focus-within)]:pointer-events-none group-[:not(:hover):not(:focus-within)]:opacity-0"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true" class="icon"><use href="/cdn/assets/sprites-core-cohbnghc.svg#ce3544" fill="currentColor"></use></svg></button></span></div></div></div></div>
<hr data-start="1898" data-end="1901">
<h2 data-start="1903" data-end="1955"><span role="text"><strong data-start="1906" data-end="1955">Simple Definition (You can use in your paper)</strong></span></h2>
<blockquote data-start="1957" data-end="2154">
<p data-start="1959" data-end="2154">A dashboard is an interactive visual interface that consolidates multiple data visualizations and key metrics into a single view, enabling users to efficiently monitor, explore, and analyze data.</p>
</blockquote>
<hr data-start="2156" data-end="2159">
<p data-start="2161" data-end="2314" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">If you want, I can help you <strong data-start="2189" data-end="2271">design exactly what your dashboard should look like (layout + charts + labels)</strong> so you can directly build it in your tool.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="z-0 flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div aria-label="Response actions"
